question: Describe the heat transfer area and structural characteristics of the steam generator.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the use of low alloy steel, stainless steel, and nickel-based alloys, all compliant with relevant BPVC Section II specifications, to ensure corrosion resistance and material compatibility in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat from the reactor after shutdown, especially when normal cooling is unavailable, without relying on external power. It ensures the reactor coolant system temperature drops below 420°F within 36 hours, maintaining fuel and pressure boundary safety limits, and its reliability and risk significance are rigorously evaluated. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and internal plenums, with the reactor vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping and systems. It details the integration of these components within the RPV, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary sides, and references other sections for specific design bases and descriptions.

edges.description: Section A describes the pressurizer materials and components within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides an overview of the RPV's internal components, including both the pressurizer and the steam generators, thus structurally connecting the two sections by listing their co-location and integration within the RPV.

Section A provides detailed material and structural information about the pressurizer, which is one of the components generally described as part of the reactor pressure vessel and its subsystems in Section B.

Section B describes the physical configuration and integration of the steam generators within the reactor coolant system, which Section A references as the heat sink for the decay heat removal system's function.

Section A describes the function and design basis of the Decay Heat Removal System, which relies on the steam generator (described structurally in Section B) as a component for removing decay heat, thus connecting the operational role of the DHRS to the physical characteristics of the steam generator.
